title: podman login to registry fails on first attempt after changes in resolv.conf
body:
/kind bug

**Description**

If the podman service is started while having a nameserver in /etc/resolv.conf, that cannot resolve the local registry, the first attempt of login fails to the registry (image push) even if resolv.conf has been updated since, as podman still tries to resolve the name based on the old resolv.conf content.
On the next attempt it succeeds, reaching out to the correct nameserver.

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1. Set /etc/resolv.conf on the host to a public DNS, which cannot resolve the registry service.
```
search cluster.local

nameserver 8.8.8.8
#nameserver 172.16.0.1
#nameserver 172.16.0.2
#nameserver 172.16.0.3
options ndots:3 timeout:1 rotate attempts:1
```
3. Start (or restart) Podman service
4. Set /etc/resolv.conf on the host to a local DNS, which can resolve the registry.
```
search cluster.local

#nameserver 8.8.8.8
nameserver 172.16.0.1
nameserver 172.16.0.2
nameserver 172.16.0.3
options ndots:3 timeout:1 rotate attempts:1
```
5. Try login to the registry.
6. Try login again.

**Describe the results you received:**
The first login attempt after podman restart or start always fails, while having correct entries in the resolv.conf
```
curl -v --unix-socket /run/podman/podman.sock http://v1.40/auth -H "accept: application/json" -H "Content-Type: application/json" -X POST -d '{"password": "<password>", "serveraddress": "harbor-harbor-core.namespace.svc", "username": "<user>"}'
* About to connect() to v1.40 port 80 (#0)
*   Trying /run/podman/podman.sock...
* Failed to set TCP_KEEPIDLE on fd 3
* Failed to set TCP_KEEPINTVL on fd 3
* Connected to v1.40 (/run/podman/podman.sock) port 80 (#0)
> POST /auth HTTP/1.1
> User-Agent: curl/7.29.0
> Host: v1.40
> accept: application/json
> Content-Type: application/json
> Content-Length: 105
>
* upload completely sent off: 105 out of 105 bytes
< HTTP/1.1 500 Internal Server Error
< Api-Version: 1.40
< Content-Type: application/json
< Libpod-Api-Version: 3.4.4
< Server: Libpod/3.4.4 (linux)
< X-Reference-Id: 0xc00062a000
< Date: Mon, 07 Mar 2022 12:25:11 GMT
< Content-Length: 263
<
{"message":"login attempt to harbor-harbor-core.namespace.svc failed with status: pinging container registry harbor-harbor-core.namespace.svc: Get \"https://harbor-harbor-core.ncms.svc/v2/\": dial tcp: lookup harbor-harbor-core.namespace.svc on 8.8.8.8:53: no such host"}
* Connection #0 to host v1.40 left intact
```
Debug logs:
```
Mar 07 12:52:50 baremetal-0 podman[387319]: time="2022-03-07T12:52:50Z" level=debug msg="GET https://harbor-harbor-core.namespace.svc/v2/"
Mar 07 12:52:50 baremetal-0 podman[387319]: time="2022-03-07T12:52:50Z" level=debug msg="Ping https://harbor-harbor-core.namespace.svc/v2/ err Get \"https://harbor-harbor-core.namespace.svc/v2/\": dial tcp: lookup harbor-harbor-core.namespace.svc on 8.8.8.8:53: no such host (&url.Error{Op:\"Get\", URL:\"https://harbor-harbor-core.namespace.svc/v2/\", Err:(*net.OpError)(0xc000821360)})"
Mar 07 12:52:50 baremetal-0 podman[387319]: time="2022-03-07T12:52:50Z" level=debug msg="GET https://harbor-harbor-core.namespace.svc/v1/_ping"
Mar 07 12:52:50 baremetal-0 podman[387319]: time="2022-03-07T12:52:50Z" level=debug msg="Ping https://harbor-harbor-core.namespace.svc/v1/_ping err Get \"https://harbor-harbor-core.namespace.svc/v1/_ping\": dial tcp: lookup harbor-harbor-core.namespace.svc on 8.8.8.8:53: no such host (&url.Error{Op:\"Get\", URL:\"https://harbor-harbor-core.namespace.svc/v1/_ping\", Err:(*net.OpError)(0xc0008214a0)})"
Mar 07 12:52:50 baremetal-0 podman[387319]: @ - - [07/Mar/2022:12:52:50 +0000] "POST /auth HTTP/1.1" 500 263 "" "curl/7.29.0"
```
**Describe the results you expected:**
On second attempt, the login succeeds, which is expected on the first attempt as well.
```
curl -v --unix-socket /run/podman/podman.sock http://v1.40/auth -H "accept: application/json" -H "Content-Type: application/json" -X POST -d '{"password": "<password>", "serveraddress": "harbor-harbor-core.namespace.svc", "username": "<user>"}'
* About to connect() to v1.40 port 80 (#0)
*   Trying /run/podman/podman.sock...
* Failed to set TCP_KEEPIDLE on fd 3
* Failed to set TCP_KEEPINTVL on fd 3
* Connected to v1.40 (/run/podman/podman.sock) port 80 (#0)
> POST /auth HTTP/1.1
> User-Agent: curl/7.29.0
> Host: v1.40
> accept: application/json
> Content-Type: application/json
> Content-Length: 105
>
* upload completely sent off: 105 out of 105 bytes
< HTTP/1.1 200 OK
< Api-Version: 1.40
< Content-Type: application/json
< Libpod-Api-Version: 3.4.4
< Server: Libpod/3.4.4 (linux)
< X-Reference-Id: 0xc0004c2000
< Date: Mon, 07 Mar 2022 12:28:49 GMT
< Content-Length: 48
<
{"IdentityToken":"","Status":"Login Succeeded"}
* Connection #0 to host v1.40 left intact
```
Debug logs:
```
Mar 07 13:00:15 baremetal-0 podman[423291]: Authenticating with existing credentials...
Mar 07 13:00:15 baremetal-0 podman[423291]: time="2022-03-07T13:00:15Z" level=debug msg="Looking for TLS certificates and private keys in /etc/docker/certs.d/harbor-harbor-core.namespace.svc"
Mar 07 13:00:15 baremetal-0 podman[423291]: time="2022-03-07T13:00:15Z" level=debug msg="GET https://harbor-harbor-core.namespace.svc/v2/"
Mar 07 13:00:15 baremetal-0 podman[423291]: time="2022-03-07T13:00:15Z" level=debug msg="Ping https://harbor-harbor-core.namespace.svc/v2/ status 401"
Mar 07 13:00:15 baremetal-0 podman[423291]: time="2022-03-07T13:00:15Z" level=debug msg="GET https://harbor-harbor-core.namespace.svc:443/service/token?account=admin&service=harbor-registry"
Mar 07 13:00:15 baremetal-0 podman[423291]: time="2022-03-07T13:00:15Z" level=debug msg="GET https://harbor-harbor-core.namespace.svc/v2/"
Mar 07 13:00:15 baremetal-0 podman[423291]: @ - - [07/Mar/2022:13:00:15 +0000] "POST /auth HTTP/1.1" 200 48 "" "curl/7.29.0"
```
**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**
The issue is consistently reproducible, and may cause issues during deployment flows or changes in resolv.conf by DHCP for example.

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
Version:      3.4.4
API Version:  3.4.4
Go Version:   go1.17.1
Built:        Thu Jan  1 00:00:00 1970
OS/Arch:      linux/amd64
```

**Have you tested with the latest version of Podman and have you checked the Podman Troubleshooting Guide? (https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/troubleshooting.md)**


Yes

**Additional environment details (AWS, VirtualBox, physical, etc.):**

Physical, and also Openstack.

comments.body: This seems like an issue with Golang itself, not Podman, as I'm quite certain we're using the standard Golang code for this.

I would figure that the podman service exited and restarted on the third attempt.  libc and probably golang does not watch for changes in /etc/resolv.conf, it is read once per start of an application.

Restarting the service is the best you can do.

> I would figure that the podman service exited and restarted on the third attempt. libc and probably golang does not watch for changes in /etc/resolv.conf, it is read once per start of an application.
> 
> Restarting the service is the best you can do.

The service does not restart between first (failed) and second(successful) attempt.
